title: vandellia obtusa
intro: Vandellia obtusa, also known as "Vanda" or "Ghayal", is a tropical plant species native to the Indian subcontinent, belonging to the family Lamiaceae. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its ability to stimulate blood flow and reduce inflammation. The plant's roots and leaves are used in various herbal preparations, including teas and infusions, to alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, while also exhibiting potential health benefits such as wound healing and antimicrobial activity. In Ayurvedic medicine, Vandellia obtusa is often combined with other herbs to create tonics and creams that promote overall well-being and skin health.
